metrics.hypothesis: the 4096-spp reference is NOT converged at high-variance frame-edge tiles, so the strict worst-tile 0.95 gate is unreachable by construction there (a delivery render is scored against a still-noisy target). Two same-config reference renders (seeds differ only) measure that self-disagreement directly.
metrics.interpretation: REFERENCE IS SELF-CONSISTENT AT THE GATE: the worst-tile SSIM between two 4096-spp renders of the SAME config (seeds differ only) is 1.0000 >= 0.95. The reference agrees with itself to within the delivery tolerance even at its noisiest tile, so residual reference Monte-Carlo noise does NOT rule out the worst-tile 0.95 gate — a delivery render that truly matched the reference could in principle clear it; the strict-gate gap is a real delivery deficit, not a noisy-target artifact.
metrics.label: MEASURED
metrics.note: REFERENCE SELF-CONSISTENCY probe on 'classroom' frame 1/4 (3840x2160), the SAME deterministic camera path as exp_render_stack.py. TWO real renders on the same box, BYTE-IDENTICAL config differing ONLY in seed (A seed=0, B seed=12345): 4096 spp fixed, adaptive OFF, denoiser OFF, guides OFF, light-tree left at the scene's .blend default — the EXACT recipe exp_render_stack.py renders its reference with (run_blender_frame is_ref=True, which FORCES use_adaptive_sampling=False and use_denoising=False and SKIPS the anchor-only light-tree lever for BOTH renders). per-tile SSIM(A,B) on the 8x8 grading grid (same _tone/_tile_rects as compute_ssim_global_and_tiles) measures the reference's SELF-CONSISTENCY: worst_tile_ref_vs_ref is how much two reference-quality renders of the SAME tile disagree at the noisiest tile. gate_reachable = (worst_tile_ref_vs_ref >= 0.95); when False the strict worst-tile gate is unreachable by construction (noise vs noise). All SSIM and wall values are MEASURED on real pixels; the comparison is valid ONLY because the two configs are identical except the seed, asserted in code before any spend.
